title: Managing Wii Backups
requirements: A dumped Wii game (ISO or split .part files)
A USB drive or SD card — an external hard drive is recommended if you're keeping more than a couple of games
cIOS and a USB loader (USB Loader GX or WiiFlow) installed on the console
_note: Covers organizing dumped Wii game backups for use with a USB loader — joining split ISO files and getting them onto a drive USB Loader GX or WiiFlow can read.

steps.title: Storage setup
steps.body:
Wii backups are usually played from **USB Loader GX** or **WiiFlow** off a USB drive or SD card. If you're storing more than a couple of games, an external hard drive is worth it.

**Format the drive as FAT32** — this is mandatory. !!Do NOT!! use exFAT, extFS, or WBFS-formatted drives. Flash drives work but are best avoided since they're slower and wear out faster than a real hard drive or SD card; SD cards are fine but also slower than a hard drive.

steps.title: Rejoin split ISO files (if needed)
steps.body:
FAT32 can't hold a single file over 4GB, so some dumping tools split Wii games into `game.part1.iso`, `game.part2.iso`, and so on. If your dump tool already outputs a single `.iso`, skip this step.

**On Windows:**
1. Put all the matching `.partX.iso` files for one game in a folder together.
2. Open a command prompt in that folder.
3. Run: `copy /b *.part?.iso game.iso`
4. Check the resulting file's checksum against what your dumping tool reported.

**On macOS/Linux:**
1. Put all the matching `.partX.iso` files for one game in a folder together.
2. Open a terminal in that folder.
3. Run: `cat $(ls *.part?.iso | sort) > game.iso`
4. Check the resulting file's checksum against what your dumping tool reported.

steps.title: Copy games with TinyWiiBackupManager
steps.body:
**TinyWiiBackupManager (TWBM)** is a free desktop app (Windows, macOS, Linux) for organizing Wii and GameCube backups on a drive, without needing to manually manage folder structures.

1. Plug your storage device into your computer.
2. Open TinyWiiBackupManager and select the **root of the drive** (not a `games` or `wbfs` subfolder).
3. Click the **+** button, or drag and drop your joined `.iso` files onto the window, to add them.
4. When done, safely eject the drive.

steps.title: Play your backups
steps.body: Plug the drive into your Wii and open **USB Loader GX** or **WiiFlow**. Both need **cIOS** installed to run Wii backups from USB or SD — see the cIOS guide if you haven't set that up yet. Select a game from the loader's menu to launch it.
